prose: This positive call rests on curated mechanistic convergence: chlorpyrifos appears as a CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence entity and as a registered stressor in a neurodegeneration-relevant AOP [E1]. This forms the evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis, not a causal claim. The molecular initiating event is biologically grounded in MitoCarta3.0 Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the proposed adverse outcome falling on the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2], lending cell-type specificity to the pathway. Supporting, though not decisive, plausibility comes from brain-penetrant structure-based predictions consistent with CNS exposure [E5] and from organophosphate-class cohort and meta-analytic signals of elevated PD risk [E4]. The 42 ToxCast assay hits illustrate the mechanistic chain but are corroboration only—anti-diagnostic and non-discriminating [E3]—so they neither strengthen nor determine the call. On balance, the curated mechanism justifies the assay_mechanism_recovered tier, with residual uncertainty in exposure magnitude and epidemiological confounding appropriately acknowledged.

citations.detail: Curated convergence: CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence AND registered neuro-AOP stressor.
MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
42 mechanistic assay hits illustrate the chain (corroboration only — anti-diagnostic, not a discriminator).
